requirementCueLines: Students in the Restoration Ecology and Environmental Horticulture (REEH) option learn and apply fundamental concepts of biology, plant science, and ecology. This disciplinary knowledge, with supporting coursework and experience, allows students to become accomplished in producing plant materials, managing sustainable landscapes, repairing damaged ecosystems, and participating in large interdisciplinary projects.
Completion Requirements
Written Communication (15 credits):
English Composition (C): one 5 credit course from the University list with a minimum 2.0 grade (ENGL 131 preferred)
Writing (W): 10 credits from the University W course list
Reasoning (RSN) (15 credits):
Calculus (10 credits): one of Q SCI 291, MATH 124, or MATH 134; one of Q SCI 292, MATH 125, or MATH 135
Statistics (5 credits): Q SCI 381 or STAT 311
Diversity (DIV) (5 credits)
Areas of Inquiry
Arts and Humanities (A&H) (10 credits):
one of COM 200, COM 202, COM 220, COM 231, LING 233/ANTH 233/COM 233, COM 234, COM 238, or COM 270
5 credits from the University A&H list
Natural Sciences (NSc) (33-42 credits):
Biology (10-15 credits) : complete one of the following:
BIOL 180, BIOL 200; either BIOL 220 or FISH 270/MARBIO 270/OCEAN 270
BIOL 161 and BIOL 162
Chemistry (10-12 credits): complete one of the following:
CHEM 120; either CHEM 220 or OCEAN 295
CHEM 142; either CHEM 152 or OCEAN 295
CHEM 143 and CHEM 153
CHEM 145 and CHEM 155
Earth Systems (3-5 credits): one of ATMOS 211, ESRM 210, ESS 201, ESS 212, or ESS 230/OCEAN 230
Additional NSc credits (10 credits): 10 credits chosen from the University NSc list (outside the major)
Social Sciences (SSc) (20 credits):
Economics (5 credits): one of ECON 200, ECON 201, ESRM 235/ENVIR 235/ECON 235, or FISH230/ECON 230
Additional SSc credits (15 credits): 10 credits chosen from the University SSc list (outside the major); 5 credits met by coursework in the major
Major Requirements
67 credits
Core Courses (22 credits): ESRM 200, ESRM 201, ESRM 250, ESRM 300/SBSE 300, ESRM 304
Restoration Ecology and Environmental Horticulture Courses (45 credits):
Electives (35 credits): see department website for list of approved courses
Capstone (10 credits): complete one of the following:
ESRM 462/T BIOL 462, ESRM 463/T BIOL 463, ESRM 464/T BIOL 464
ESRM 494 and ESRM 495
ESRM 494 and ESRM 496
Additional electives as needed to reach 180 credits
